notes: Mix of MCQ, code, SQL, debugging, case-study, and tool tasks
Every practical task includes AI scoring rubric

questions.stem: What best describes the kind of work you do or want to do?
questions.context: We'll use your answer to tailor the rest of the assessment.

B.text: Work with data, analytics, or AI models

C.text: Design visuals, interfaces, or creative content

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: High — first question for all users

questions.stem: Fix the bug in this React component that causes an infinite re-render loop.
questions.context: A junior developer wrote a component that fetches user data. The page freezes when opened.

practical_task.expected_output: Component renders user name without infinite loop. useEffect has [userId] dependency array.
constraints: Must handle the loading state correctly

rubric.strong_evidence: Adds [userId] to useEffect
Uses AbortController or cleanup function
Adds try/catch or .catch()
Clean, readable fix with comments

partial_credit_rules: Award 50% if dependency array added but empty
Award 75% if correct dep but no cleanup

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the user's code fix. Check: (1) Did they add [userId] to useEffect dependency array? (2) Did they add cleanup/abort logic? (3) Did they add error handling? Return JSON with score, detected_skills, missing_skills, category_score_deltas, confidence, feedback, follow_up_question.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: High — React is top frontend skill in IT jobs

questions.stem: Write the CSS to create a responsive navigation bar that collapses into a hamburger menu on mobile.
questions.context: The nav has 5 links. On desktop they show horizontally. On screens below 768px, they should stack vertically behind a toggle button.

practical_task.expected_output: Desktop: horizontal nav. Mobile: hidden links with hamburger visible.

rubric.criterion: Uses flexbox or grid for layout
rubric.strong_evidence: display:flex with justify-content
@media (max-width:768px) with correct breakpoint
rubric.weak_evidence: Hardcoded pixel widths without media query

rubric.strong_evidence: hamburger display:block on mobile, links hidden
Logical class names, no !important abuse

partial_credit_rules: 50% for correct desktop but broken mobile
75% if mobile works but hamburger missing

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the CSS solution. Check: flexbox/grid usage, media query correctness, hamburger toggle, code quality. Return JSON with score, detected_skills, category_score_deltas, feedback.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: High — CSS/responsive is required in 90%+ frontend jobs

questions.stem: Design a REST API for a simple task management app. Define the endpoints, HTTP methods, request/response bodies, and status codes.
questions.context: The app needs: create task, list all tasks, get single task, update task, delete task. Tasks have: id, title, description, status (todo/in_progress/done), created_at.

practical_task.expected_output: A well-structured REST API spec with proper HTTP methods, URL patterns, status codes, and request/response schemas.
constraints: Must use proper HTTP status codes

rubric.strong_evidence: GET/POST/PUT-PATCH/DELETE mapped correctly
/api/tasks, /api/tasks/:id with consistent naming
200, 201, 204, 400, 404 used correctly
JSON schemas with types defined
Consistent error response format with messages

red_flags: No distinction between PUT and PATCH
partial_credit_rules: 60% if endpoints correct but no schemas
80% if schemas present but status codes wrong

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the API design. Check REST conventions, URL structure, HTTP methods, status codes, schemas, error handling. Return JSON with score, detected_skills, feedback.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: High — REST API design is core backend skill

questions.stem: Write SQL to find the top 3 best-selling products per category, including their total revenue, for the last 30 days.
questions.context: Tables: products(id, name, category_id, price), orders(id, product_id, quantity, order_date), categories(id, name). Only include categories with at least 5 orders.

practical_task.expected_output: Result set with columns: category_name, product_name, total_revenue, rank
constraints: Must handle the top-3 per category correctly

rubric.strong_evidence: Uses RANK() or ROW_NUMBER() with PARTITION BY category
Proper JOIN between products, orders, categories with SUM
WHERE order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
rubric.weak_evidence: No date filter or hardcoded date

rubric.strong_evidence: HAVING COUNT(*) >= 5 on category group
category_name, product_name, total_revenue, rank all present

partial_credit_rules: 50% for correct joins but wrong window function
75% if ranking works but no date filter

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the SQL query. Check: window function usage, correct joins, date filtering, HAVING clause, output format. Return JSON with score, detected_skills, feedback.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: High — SQL analytics is the #1 skill for data analysts
Medium — SQL is common in backend roles

questions.stem: You trained a classification model and got these results. Analyze the confusion matrix and recommend improvements.
questions.context:
Binary classifier for loan default prediction.
Confusion Matrix:
              Predicted No  Predicted Yes
Actual No         850          50
Actual Yes        120          80

Total samples: 1100. Positive class = 'Yes' (default).

practical_task.expected_output: Analysis identifying class imbalance, low recall for positive class, and actionable recommendations.
constraints: Must identify the business impact of false negatives
edge_cases: Cost asymmetry between FP and FN

rubric.strong_evidence: Precision=0.615, Recall=0.4, F1=0.485, Accuracy=0.845
Notes 900:200 imbalance and its effect on metrics
Explains cost of missing defaults (false negatives) vs false alarms
Suggests SMOTE, threshold tuning, cost-sensitive learning, or collecting more positive samples

red_flags: Claims 84.5% accuracy means the model is good
partial_credit_rules: 50% for correct metrics but no business analysis
75% for good analysis but weak recommendations

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate the analysis. Check metric calculations, imbalance detection, business reasoning, actionable recommendations. Return JSON with score, detected_skills, feedback.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: High — model evaluation is core DS/ML skill

questions.stem: Classify the following transactions and prepare the journal entries.
questions.context:
Your company had these transactions this month:
1. Purchased office supplies for ETB 5,000 cash
2. Received ETB 50,000 from a client for services rendered
3. Paid monthly rent of ETB 15,000
4. Bought a delivery vehicle for ETB 200,000 on credit
5. Owner withdrew ETB 10,000 for personal use

practical_task.expected_output: Five correct double-entry journal entries with proper debit/credit classification.
constraints: Must identify account types (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense)
edge_cases: Owner withdrawal is equity, not expense

rubric.strong_evidence: All 5 entries have correct DR/CR sides
Vehicle = Asset, Supplies = Expense or Asset, Withdrawal = Drawing/Equity
rubric.weak_evidence: Misclassifies vehicle as expense or withdrawal as expense

rubric.strong_evidence: Date, account, DR amount, CR amount in standard journal format
Every entry balances (DR = CR)

partial_credit_rules: 60% if 3/5 entries correct
80% if all entries correct but format is informal

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate journal entries. Check: correct DR/CR, account classification (asset/liability/equity/revenue/expense), format, balancing. Return JSON with score, detected_skills, feedback.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: Very High — accounting is the #1 job in Accounting category (655 jobs)

questions.stem: Given this trial balance, prepare a simple Income Statement and Balance Sheet.
questions.context:
Trial Balance (ETB):
Cash: 120,000 DR | Accounts Receivable: 45,000 DR | Supplies: 8,000 DR | Equipment: 180,000 DR | Accounts Payable: 35,000 CR | Owner Capital: 200,000 CR | Service Revenue: 95,000 CR | Salaries Expense: 40,000 DR | Rent Expense: 18,000 DR | Utilities Expense: 9,000 DR | Owner Drawing: 10,000 DR
Total DR: 430,000 | Total CR: 330,000+100,000=430,000

practical_task.expected_output: Income Statement showing Net Income = 28,000 and Balance Sheet that balances.
constraints: Must separate revenue and expenses correctly

rubric.strong_evidence: Revenue 95k - Expenses 67k = Net Income 28k
Assets=353k, Liabilities+Equity=353k, balanced
Shows Net Income added to Owner Capital minus Drawings
Standard financial statement format with headers

partial_credit_rules: 50% for correct IS but wrong BS
75% if both correct but formatting poor

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate financial statements. Check: IS correctness, BS balance, net income flow to equity, formatting. Return JSON.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: Very High — Senior Accountant has 361 jobs

questions.stem: Analyze this sales pipeline and recommend which deals to prioritize this week.
questions.context:
You have 5 active deals:
1. ABC Corp — ETB 500,000 — Proposal sent 3 weeks ago — No response to 2 follow-ups
2. XYZ Ltd — ETB 120,000 — Demo completed yesterday — Very enthusiastic
3. DEF Inc — ETB 800,000 — Initial meeting scheduled Friday — New lead
4. GHI Co — ETB 200,000 — Verbal agreement — Waiting for PO
5. JKL Corp — ETB 50,000 — Price objection raised — Competitor involved

Your weekly target is ETB 300,000 in closed deals.

practical_task.expected_output: Prioritized action plan focusing on GHI (close), XYZ (advance), and JKL (overcome objection).
constraints: Must give specific actions for each deal
edge_cases: Risk of losing JKL to competitor

rubric.strong_evidence: GHI first (closest to close), XYZ second (hot lead), then JKL
Call GHI to expedite PO, send XYZ proposal same day, prepare competitive analysis for JKL
Notes that ABC may be dead, DEF needs qualification before investing time
Shows GHI+XYZ = 320k which exceeds 300k target

partial_credit_rules: 50% for correct priority but no action items
75% for good actions but wrong priority order

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate sales pipeline analysis. Check: prioritization logic, action specificity, pipeline stage awareness, target alignment. Return JSON.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: Very High — Sales has 423+ jobs in dataset

questions.stem: Your manager is out of office next week and has 12 meetings, 3 travel bookings, and 5 document reviews pending. Create a priority action plan for handling their absence.
questions.context: You are the Executive Assistant. The manager will have limited phone access. Two of the meetings are with the CEO. One travel booking is international and departs Monday morning.

practical_task.expected_output: Structured priority plan with immediate actions (travel), high-priority (CEO meetings), and delegatable items.
constraints: Must identify what can be rescheduled vs delegated vs needs manager approval
edge_cases: CEO meetings cannot be rescheduled easily

rubric.strong_evidence: International travel first (Monday departure), CEO meetings next
Identifies which meetings can be attended by another team member
Draft out-of-office reply, brief stakeholders, set up forwarding
Categorizes reviews by deadline, preps summaries for manager's quick review

partial_credit_rules: 50% for correct priorities but no delegation
75% if delegation plan but no communication plan

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate admin workflow plan. Check: urgency prioritization, delegation, communication, document handling. Return JSON.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: Very High — Secretary (292) + Receptionist (255) are top admin jobs

questions.stem: Write the Excel formulas to complete this monthly expense tracker.
questions.context:
You have a spreadsheet with columns: A=Date, B=Category (Office/Travel/Supplies/Other), C=Description, D=Amount. Row 2-50 have data.

Write formulas for:
1. Cell E1: Total of all expenses
2. Cell F1: Average expense amount
3. Cell G1: Count of 'Travel' expenses only
4. Cell H1: Largest single expense
5. Cell E2: Running total (cumulative sum) for row 2

practical_task.expected_output: E1: =SUM(D2:D50), F1: =AVERAGE(D2:D50), G1: =COUNTIF(B2:B50,"Travel"), H1: =MAX(D2:D50), E2: =SUM(D$2:D2)

rubric.strong_evidence: =SUM(D2:D50) correct range
=AVERAGE(D2:D50)
=COUNTIF(B2:B50,"Travel")
=MAX(D2:D50)
=SUM(D$2:D2) with mixed reference for drag-down
rubric.weak_evidence: Hardcoded sum or no mixed reference

partial_credit_rules: 60% for 3/5 correct
80% for all correct but running total without mixed reference

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate Excel formulas. Check: SUM, AVERAGE, COUNTIF, MAX, running total with mixed references. Return JSON.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: Very High — Excel is required in admin and accounting roles
High — Excel is core accounting tool

questions.stem: Critique this design brief and suggest improvements to the proposed brand identity.
questions.context:
A startup called 'FreshBite' (healthy meal delivery) wants:
- Logo: Green leaf icon + the word FreshBite in Comic Sans
- Colors: Neon green (#00FF00) and black (#000000)
- Target audience: Health-conscious professionals aged 25-45
- Tagline: 'Eat Fresh or Die'

Identify at least 3 design problems and propose specific fixes.

practical_task.expected_output: Identifies Comic Sans as unprofessional, neon green as harsh, tagline as inappropriate. Proposes modern typography, natural green palette, refined tagline.
constraints: Must address typography, color, and messaging
Must give specific alternatives, not just criticism
edge_cases: Accessibility of neon green on mobile

rubric.strong_evidence: Identifies Comic Sans as unprofessional, suggests alternatives (Montserrat, Poppins, Lato)
Notes neon green is harsh/inaccessible, suggests earth tones or muted greens (#4CAF50, #2E7D32)
Notes tagline is aggressive/off-brand, suggests alternatives like 'Fresh meals, delivered daily'
Structured critique with clear reasoning and brand-audience alignment

red_flags: Cannot identify Comic Sans as a problem
partial_credit_rules: 50% for identifying problems but no solutions
75% for good solutions but poor reasoning

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate design critique. Check: typography awareness, color theory, messaging/branding, professionalism. Return JSON.

job_evidence.dataset_frequency_note: Very High — Graphics Designer is #1 creative role (342+186+175 jobs)

questions.stem: Describe step-by-step how you would set up and draw a basic floor plan in AutoCAD for a 10m x 12m single-room office.

questions.stem: You've built a model with 92% accuracy. Your PM asks you to ship it to production by Friday. What are the first 3 things you check or do?
questions.context: The model predicts customer churn. It was trained in a Jupyter notebook on a sampled dataset.

practical_task.expected_output: DS focuses on validation/bias/metrics; MLE focuses on serving/latency/containerization.
edge_cases: Both are valid — the focus reveals the role

rubric.strong_evidence: Mentions test on full dataset, checks for data leakage, evaluates on different segments
Mentions containerization, API endpoint, latency testing, monitoring
Mentions both validation AND deployment concerns

partial_credit_rules: If mostly validation focus → data-scientist signal
If mostly production focus → ml-engineer signal

questions.ai_evaluation_prompt: Evaluate answer to determine DS vs MLE orientation. Check if focus is on validation/metrics (DS) or deployment/serving (MLE). Return JSON with category_score_deltas.

questions.stem: A critical bug is found: the checkout button submits but the order doesn't appear in the database. Where do you start debugging?
questions.context: Full-stack e-commerce app with React frontend and Node.js/Express backend.

A.text: Open browser DevTools, check Network tab for the POST request status and payload

B.text: Check the backend logs for the /api/orders endpoint, verify the database query

C.text: Check the Network tab first, then trace to the backend endpoint and DB query — follow the full request lifecycle

D.text: Clear the browser cache and try again

questions.stem: Write a Dockerfile to containerize a basic Node.js Express application.

CMD ["node", "app.js"] or ["npm", "start"]
